stories.headline: Bitcoin stalls at $79.4K as Coinbase premium flips negative for first time since April 8
stories.tldr: BTC's rally faded near $79,400 and slipped to ~$77K as US institutional demand cooled into the FOMC decision.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: A negative Coinbase premium plus price under the $79.2K short-term holder cost basis sets up a fresh bid-test for risk assets the moment Powell speaks.
the_details: BTC dropped from $79,400 toward $77,000 as the Coinbase premium index turned negative for the first time since April 8.
Price fell below the $79,200 short-term holder average cost basis, a level that historically triggers reflexive selling.
ETH traded at $2,322.95, down 0.7% on the day, with traders eyeing $2,800-$3,000 if the Fed signals dovish.

stories.headline: April crypto exploits hit $606M in 18 days, 3.7x the entire Q1 2026 total
stories.tldr: Eighteen days of April have erased $606.2M to hacks, dwarfing Q1's $165.5M and reframing 2026's security narrative.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: The pace forces protocols, custodians, and insurers to reprice cross-chain risk in real time, with knock-on effects for DeFi TVL and bridge usage.
the_details: Cumulative April losses reached $606.2M by April 18, versus $165.5M for all of Q1 2026.
The Drift Protocol $285M exploit on April 1 and Kelp DAO's $292M hit on April 19 anchor the surge.
Smaller hits include CoW Swap's $1.2M domain hijack on April 14 and a $13.74M drain at Russia-linked Grinex.

stories.headline: Aave rallies DeFi consortium to backstop Kelp DAO's $292M cross-chain exploit
stories.tldr: Aave is leading a DeFi partner push to contain fallout from the $292M Kelp DAO hack as TVL bled from $26.4B to ~$20B.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: A coordinated industry bailout, even informal, is a stress test for whether DeFi can self-insure cross-chain bridge risk without sovereign or CEX backstops.
the_details: Hacker tricked LayerZero's EndpointV2 contract into releasing 116,500 rsETH from the Kelp bridge.
Aave token fell over 18% on the weekend as depositors yanked funds, prompting the bailout coordination.
DeFi TVL dropped from $26.4B on April 18 to roughly $20B by April 19 morning, per DefiLlama.

stories.headline: Israel approves first regulated stablecoin BILS, built natively on Solana
stories.tldr: Israeli regulators greenlit BILS, the country's first regulated stablecoin, developed in partnership with the Solana network.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Sovereign-grade stablecoin issuance on Solana strengthens the chain's pitch as the default payments rail and pressures Ethereum L2s on policy capture.
the_details: BILS is Israel's first stablecoin to receive formal regulatory approval, per CoinDesk reporting.
The token is engineered on Solana, leveraging sub-second finality enabled by the Alpenglow upgrade.
Approval lands the same week Western Union unveiled USDPT, also Solana-based, signaling chain consolidation for FX rails.

stories.headline: Solana Company raises $8M from Mirae Asset and HashKey Capital
stories.tldr: Solana Company closed an $8M strategic round backed by Mirae Asset and HashKey Capital, doubling down on Asia distribution.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Tier-1 Korean and Hong Kong capital tilts Solana's growth axis toward Asia just as Firedancer targets a 1M TPS jump.
the_details: Mirae Asset and HashKey Capital led the $8M strategic share sale.
SOL trades near $86.47 with a $49.8B market cap, ranked 7th by market cap.
Firedancer upgrade is targeting throughput from ~1,300 to 1M TPS over the course of 2026.
